articles.title: Republicans Delay Budget Votes as They Balk at Trump’s Fund
articles.summary: The G.O.P. abandoned its plan to quickly pass a $72 billion immigration crackdown bill as senators revolted over his plan to pay people who claim government persecution.

articles.title: Thousands of Miles From the Iran War, Asia’s Currencies Feel the Strain
articles.summary: Soaring oil prices and a surging dollar are testing Asia’s foreign-exchange reserves, which were built up after the 1997 crisis.

articles.title: San Diego Mosque Shooter So Alarmed Police, They Seized Father’s Guns
articles.summary: In 2025, Caleb Vazquez’s obsession with mass shooters and Nazism prompted reports to the authorities. His father surrendered dozens of firearms.

articles.title: Senate Republicans rebel against Trump's $1.8 trillion fund
articles.summary: In a rare rebuke, Senate Republicans postponed a vote to fund immigration enforcement amid alarm over the novel fund

articles.title: Palestinians drop bid for a senior U.N. role after U.S. pressure
articles.summary: The Palestinian delegation to the United Nations has dropped its bid to secure a top U.N. job after the U.S. threatens to revoke their visas.

articles.title: GOP races to fund immigration enforcement. And, U.S. indicts former Cuban president
articles.summary: Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.

articles.title: In San Diego, a Final Prayer Before Laying Heroes to Rest
articles.summary: Thousands of mourners filled a San Diego park to recite a funeral prayer for the three men killed by gunmen at a mosque on Monday.

articles.title: Iran and Oman in Talks Over Strait of Hormuz Ship Payment System
articles.summary: The discussions suggest that the United States and the Iranian government may not be close to reaching a deal to end a war that has badly damaged the global economy.

articles.title: Audit Immunity for Trump Family Puts I.R.S. in a Bind
articles.summary: Federal law prohibits the Internal Revenue Service from halting an audit at the direction of the president or his aides.

articles.title: GOP Pulls Measure on Trump’s War Powers in Iran, Lacking Votes to Defeat It
articles.summary: House Republican leaders abruptly scrapped a planned vote on a measure to direct President Trump to end the conflict or win authorization for it, amid party defections and absences.

articles.title: NASCAR superstar Kyle Busch dies at 41 after hospitalization with 'severe illness'
articles.summary: Kyle Busch, who won more races than anyone across NASCAR's three national series, has died at 41. A joint statement from the Busch Family, Richard Childress Racing and NASCAR said he died after being

articles.title: Inside the Senate G.O.P. Meltdown Over Trump’s Fund
articles.summary: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, went to Capitol Hill to allay Republicans’ concerns over a fund to pay people who claim government mistreatment. It did not go well.

articles.title: Trump's Raúl Castro indictment sends a signal to Cuba
articles.summary: The indictment of Raul Castro is the latest move by the Trump administration to ratchet up pressure on the Cuban government.

articles.title: Trump's towering arch clears another federal hurdle, despite public pushback
articles.summary: The Commission of Fine Arts gave the arch design its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components. The president said Thursday that he doesn't need approval from Congress.

articles.title: Republican congressman explains his objections to $1.8B Anti-Weaponization Fund
articles.summary: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., about his concerns with the so-called Anti-Weaponization Fund.

articles.title: American passenger feels 'betrayed' by federal order to stay in hantavirus quarantine
articles.summary: Two passengers from the cruise ship that had a hantavirus outbreak were blocked by the federal government from leaving the quarantine unit. One says she is being detained against her will.

articles.title: Democrats wanted answers for what went wrong in 2024. Now, there are more questions
articles.summary: The Democratic Party wanted a review of the 2024 election. Then, the DNC learned the report was incomplete and unverifiable, party chairman Ken Martin said Thursday, releasing an annotated version.

articles.title: Abortion pills "just in case"? Planned Parenthood will offer them in two states
articles.summary: In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.

articles.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
articles.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

articles.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
articles.summary: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.

articles.title: Netanyahu scolds Israeli security minister for videos taunting flotilla activists
articles.summary: Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.

articles.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
articles.summary: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable plac

articles.title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
articles.summary: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
